signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 158.82; safe-haven bid competes with 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: HV30 at 7.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 0.7 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.08: mild put skew. CFTC LF net long +41,675 (week Apr 14), long%=72.0%; AM not reducing. AUD/USD at 0.7179, price stalling — late-trend caution signal. Watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426; AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3538 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: USD/CAD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoC at 2.25% (ON HOLD) vs Fed at 3.75% (ON HOLD) — 150bp CAD carry disadvantage. USD/CAD at 1.3643, testing the 1.3650 level. Positioning net short (CFTC, week ending Apr 14). The technical level and CB differential suggest structural USD/CAD upside; watch for a break above 1.3650.
